column1: How the coach AI handles a mid-program request to change an exercise. Every swap is resolved by walking the Substitutions sheet in priority order and returning the FIRST entry that passes the user's equipment, skill and exclusion filters, never by inventing an exercise. A swap changes which exercise fills a slot, never the slot's safety envelope: the new exercise is re-prescribed from Prescription Logic and re-clamped by Safety Rules and its own Min RIR. Every swap writes to swap_history; dislike and pain swaps also update excluded_exercise_ids. A reported stop symptom is never a swap, it is an S06 escalation.

column3: User taps swap or tells the coach they dislike it
column4: Walk the Substitutions list for that exercise in priority order. Return the first entry that passes the user's equipment tags, skill level and exclusions. Serve it in the same slot with the same slot prescription.
column6: Permanent. Add from_id to excluded_exercise_ids so it is never served again unless the user re-enables it.

column2: exercise hurts (incl. acute sharp joint pain)
column3: User says it is uncomfortable or aggravates a known area, or reports acute sharp joint pain in a joint during the movement
column4: Same as SW01, and additionally skip any substitute whose Stress Regions overlap the user's affected_regions, and any sharing the flagged movement pattern. Prefer Supported=Yes and Impact=Low substitutes. Region matching is now structured (Exercise Database Stress Regions vs users.affected_regions), not inferred from names. At program-build time this is driven by the Injury Modifications sheet; mid-program pain swaps use the same region rows.
column6: Permanent block on from_id pending user confirmation the area is fine. Logs a pain_flag.

column3: User reports chest pain, dizziness, faintness, unusual breathlessness, palpitations or numbness
column4: Do NOT swap and continue. Trigger Safety Rule S06: end the session, show the stop message, halt further generation until resolved or cleared. This overrides any swap request.
column6: Escalation, not a swap. Writes a stop_symptom_event to flags.

column3: Chosen exercise needs a machine or tool the user cannot access right now
column4: Walk Substitutions for the first entry whose required_equipment_tags are a subset of the user's available tags. Temporary swaps (busy gym) are not added to exclusions.
column6: Session-only unless the user changes their stored equipment, then permanent for the program.

column3: User cannot complete the prescribed reps with acceptable form, or reports the movement is beyond them
column4: First reduce load or reps within the slot (see Progression Engine down-adjust). If the exercise itself is the problem, walk Substitutions to the first entry at or below the user's skill level and, for compounds, prefer a more supported or machine variation.
column6: Temporary. Re-offers the original once progression_state shows the user has advanced.

column3: User finds it trivial even at target load and top of the rep range
column4: First progress load or reps (see Progression Engine up-adjust). Only swap the exercise if the user specifically wants variety, in which case offer a same-group substitute of equal or higher skill.

column3: User names an exercise they want in a slot
column4: If the named exercise exists in the database, passes their equipment and skill filters, and fits the slot's muscle_group and type filters, place it. If it fails a HARD_SAFETY rule or the slot filter, explain why in one sentence and offer the closest valid option.
column6: Honoured where safe. Never overrides screening, skill gates or equipment reality.

column3: User is bored, no specific target
column4: Rotate one or two slots to same-group substitutes the user has not done recently, keeping the day's muscle and volume targets intact.
column6: No exclusion. Freshness tracked so the same swaps are not repeated.
